cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is NOT a reason the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. Looking back at the passage provided, it says: "The English language was first introduced to the Americas by British colonization... Similarly, the language spread to numerous other parts of the world as a result of British trade and colonization elsewhere and the spread of the former British Empire..." So the reasons mentioned are British colonization, British trade, and the spread of the former British Empire. The options given are: (A) British colonization – this is mentioned as a reason, so not the answer. (B) the spread of the former English Empire – the passage says "the spread of the former British Empire," so this is a reason, not the answer. (C) British trade around the world – this is mentioned as a reason, so not the answer. (D) a large British population – the passage does mention the British Empire's population in 1921, but it's not stated that the large population itself
